answer:
In Cynewulf's *Juliana*, the imprisoned saint confronts a demon (disguised as an angel of God) who attempts to tempt her into sacrificing to the pagan idols and thus escaping further torment.[^1] A divine voice instructs her to seize and bind the intruder until it reveals its true nature and origins.[^2] She does so, then relentlessly interrogates the fiend, forcing it to confess its role in biblical evils (such as inciting Judas, Herod, Pilate, Nero, and others), its hierarchical subordination to the "king of hell," its methods of tempting the righteous through lust, delusion, and sin, and its countless historical crimes against humanity from Adam and Eve onward.[^3]

Juliana's direct speech in this extended confrontation consists primarily of commanding questions that compel the demon's humiliating admissions:

> “You must tell me further,  
> fiend of mankind, of your journey,  
> and who sent you to me.”[^4]

> “You must tell me further,  
> fiend of souls, how you most harm the righteous  
> through the slide of sin, you who are steeped in deceit.”[^5]

> “Tell me, wretched creature,  
> unclean spirit, how you, ruler of darkness,  
> dare to join the company of the clean?  
> You once strove against Christ, faithless one,  
> and waged war, plotted against the holy.  
> The pit of hell was dug deep for you,  
> where you, in your pride, sought a dwelling in distress.  
> I thought you would have become more wary  
> of such meetings with the righteous, and less bold,  
> you who have often been thwarted by the King of glory.”[^6]

> “You must confess more of your evil deeds,  
> lowly hell-spirit, before you may go from here,  
> what great and wicked works you have wrought  
> to the ruin of mankind with your dark delusions.”[^7]

She later drags the bound demon before the pagan judge Eleusius (humiliating it further) before releasing it to return to hell.[^8]

[^1]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-a370d7).

[^2]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-28b423).

[^3]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraphs](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-5f858b).

[^4]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-bd327c).

[^5]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-def3ab).

[^6]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-579347).

[^7]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-d3a506).

[^8]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-6b7e11).
